output:
I’m locating the implementation for story `21-1-extract-workflow-actors` and the relevant diff first, then I’ll review it against correctness, security, architecture, and whatever AC evidence exists.I found the implementation commit for the story. Next I’m reading the story artifact and the code diff around `8d7a529` to assess whether the extraction was correct and whether the proof’s noted failures are still real issues.I have the extraction diff. There’s already one suspicious point: the story artifact claims `workflow-actors.ts` is under 200 lines, but the implementation commit summary shows the file as 206 lines in the patch. I’m checking the current files, imports, and architecture notes now to separate real defects from proof drift.The structural checks now match the updated proof: `workflow-actors.ts` is 199 lines and the dependency direction matches the architecture doc. I’m running targeted validation around the extracted module and its consumers to confirm there isn’t a hidden regression behind the refactor.The extracted module and its direct machine tests pass. One remaining issue surfaced in lint: `workflow-machine.ts` still imports `EngineEvent` as a local type and never uses it, so the implementation is not lint-clean as reviewed.`tsc` shows no errors in the new extraction files themselves; the only type errors in this area are in [`src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts), which matches the story’s allowance for pre-existing unrelated type issues. I’m checking whether the lint warning and any API drift should count as findings against the story.No blocking findings.

The extraction is correct and architecture-aligned. [`src/lib/workflow-actors.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts) now contains the dispatch/null-task actor logic, [`src/lib/workflow-types.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-types.ts) holds shared boundary types with no runtime code, and [`src/lib/workflow-machine.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts) depends on those modules in the direction required by AD6. I found no security issues in the extraction itself, and the direct consumer tests passed: `src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts` plus `src/lib/__tests__/workflow-engine.test.ts` ran clean with 171/171 tests passing.

AC coverage also checks out in the current tree: [`src/lib/workflow-actors.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts) is 199 lines, [`src/lib/workflow-machine.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts) is 1102 lines, there is no `workflow-machine` reference in the actors module, and the story proof in [`verification/21-1-extract-workflow-actors-proof.md`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/verification/21-1-extract-workflow-actors-proof.md) is consistent with spot checks. Residual note only: the current branch still has a non-blocking lint warning for an unused `EngineEvent` import at [`src/lib/workflow-machine.ts:47`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts#L47), but that does not amount to a correctness, security, or architecture failure for this story.

<verdict>pass</verdict>
